title: [v4.4.1-rhel] builds try to write to additionalImageStore
body:
... read-only locataion

This vendors in c/storage v1.45.7 to address this issue in RHEL 8.8/9.2: containers/storage#1733

This addresses: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-41079 and https://issues.redhat.com/browse/ACCELFIX-243

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.4.1-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Capturing the initial error.  I think I've worked around it with the added test pick commits, but just in case, here's the starting one.

@edsantiago, if this passes, could you please take a good eyeball at the third commit, "Change images_test expect from 10 to 9," and let me know if that's problematic? I'm just slightly leary that we might have introduced a change that would break something elsewhere in this release.
Looks like you also need to get https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21875 here like you did in the 4.9 PR (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23255)

> @edsantiago, if this passes, could you please take a good eyeball at the third commit, "Change images_test expect from 10 to 9," and let me know if that's problematic? I'm just slightly leary that we might have introduced a change that would break something elsewhere in this release.

@TomSweeneyRedHat That is fine you cherry-pick cleanup was incorrect. If you look at the original commit it changed from 9 -> 10, you changed the commit to go from 8 -> 10 thus failing the test.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20452/commits/85cc4092fc8b73b5e3f962c2305e00acd02cb734

Therefore 8 -> 9 is correct here.
Thanks, @Luap99. Yes, both comments are correct.
@Luap99 @edsantiago Thanks!  I added the mount test fix and it's all happy green test buttons now.  Merge me please!
